body.question_1: Reflecting on the full academic year, how would you rate your overall educational experience at this university?
body.answer_1: My overall academic year experience in the Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning program at the School of Information Technology has unfortunately been problematic and has fallen considerably short of the expectations I had when I enrolled. Persistent issues with teaching quality, administrative responsiveness, infrastructure reliability, and student support have collectively impacted my ability to learn and thrive in this environment. I feel that despite making this feedback heard through multiple channels, meaningful change has been slow and the institution's response to student concerns has lacked urgency and accountability. I sincerely hope the upcoming academic year brings substantive improvements in all the areas I have identified, not only for my own benefit but for all students who will follow in future cohorts.

body.question_2: How effectively do you believe this year's coursework has prepared you for your future career or further academic studies?
body.answer_2: Career preparation support from the Career Center at the School of Information Technology has been problematic and has left many students — including myself — feeling ill-equipped for the competitive graduate employment market. Career workshops are infrequent, poorly promoted, and often scheduled at times that conflict with lecture commitments, reducing student participation. The internship database is outdated and contains many positions that are no longer active, and support for students during internship placements is minimal to nonexistent. I raise concerns about the university to substantially reinvest in the Career Center — expanding its employer network, modernizing its tools, and increasing the frequency and quality of career development programming across all academic programs and year levels.

body.question_3: How would you assess the progression of your academic skills and knowledge throughout this academic year?
body.answer_3: The curriculum for my Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning program at the School of Information Technology is well-designed and clearly aligned with current industry demands and emerging professional trends in the field. Course structures are logical, building progressively on foundational concepts before introducing advanced topics, which gives students a solid intellectual scaffold. The balance between theoretical foundations and practical application is excellent, with project-based assessments, case studies, and laboratory work that directly reflect real-world professional scenarios I will encounter in my career. I particularly value the inclusion of contemporary tools and technologies, which ensures graduates are competitive and well-prepared for the demands of the modern job market.

body.question_4: How satisfied are you with the extracurricular activities, student clubs, and personal development opportunities available?
body.answer_4: The extracurricular opportunities available at the School of Information Technology, coordinated by the Student Affairs Office, are outstanding and represent a genuinely important complement to the formal academic curriculum. A wide and diverse range of student clubs, cultural societies, sports teams, and volunteer programs are actively supported and well-resourced by the university, enabling students to develop leadership skills, build social connections, and pursue interests outside their academic programs. Events are well-promoted, regularly scheduled, and inclusive in their design, welcoming students from all backgrounds and programs to participate equally. I value the institution's recognition that holistic student development extends beyond classroom performance and into every dimension of campus life.

body.question_5: How well has the university's Career Center prepared you for internship opportunities and graduate employment?
body.answer_5: The career preparation support provided by the Career Center at the School of Information Technology has been impressive and has made a tangible difference to my professional readiness and employment prospects as I approach graduation. Resume writing workshops, mock interviews, and industry networking events have been well-organized, highly practical, and attended by credible industry professionals who provided genuinely actionable feedback. The internship matching program is effective, with clear communication about available placements and strong follow-up support for students during their internship periods. I applaud the Career Center's proactive approach and its strong relationships with employers, which have opened real opportunities for students across all programs.

body.question_6: How would you rate the university's financial services, including tuition fee management and scholarship support?
body.answer_6: Financial services at the School of Information Technology, managed by the Finance Department, operate at a fair level that fulfills basic requirements without particular distinction in either efficiency or student-centeredness. Tuition fee management and billing are generally accurate, though occasional discrepancies require follow-up that can be time-consuming. Scholarship and financial aid communication could be significantly improved — students often report uncertainty about disbursement timelines and eligibility criteria that creates unnecessary anxiety during critical financial planning periods. Greater transparency through automated notifications, a clearer scholarship portal, and improved staff responsiveness to financial queries would substantially raise the standard of financial services for the student community.

body.question_7: How would you evaluate the IT infrastructure and digital services provided by the university over the course of the year?
body.answer_7: The IT infrastructure and digital learning environment provided by the IT Support Department is outstanding and has greatly supported my studies at the School of Information Technology this semester. Campus Wi-Fi coverage is consistently strong and reliable across all academic buildings, laboratories, and study spaces, enabling seamless online collaboration and research activities throughout the day. Computer labs are well-equipped with up-to-date hardware and licensed software relevant to my field, reducing the burden on students to source tools independently. The IT helpdesk is responsive and knowledgeable, resolving technical issues promptly and minimizing disruption to student workflows. I recognize the university's ongoing investment in digital infrastructure.

body.question_8: How satisfied are you with the library resources — both physical and digital — that were available throughout the academic year?
body.answer_8: Learning resources for my Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning program at the School of Information Technology are moderate, with some courses providing excellent, well-curated materials and others offering minimal guidance beyond the core required textbook. The library's digital collection is reasonable in scope but has some notable gaps in specialized resources relevant to advanced topics in our field. The LMS functions adequately as a distribution platform, though its use varies significantly between lecturers, creating an inconsistent experience across modules. A more standardized approach to resource provision, with minimum content requirements per course, would considerably improve the consistency and quality of the learning environment.

body.question_9: How effectively did the university communicate important information, deadlines, and updates throughout the academic year?
body.answer_9: The academic administrative processes at the School of Information Technology, overseen by the Academic Affairs Office, have been a source of considerable frustration for many students this semester. Course registration was plagued by system errors and conflicting timetables that took weeks to resolve, causing students to miss early enrollment advantages. Grade dispute processes are opaque and slow, with students waiting over a month without updates or acknowledgment of their submissions. Communication from administrative offices is reactive rather than proactive, and staff sometimes provide inconsistent information depending on who is contacted. I recommend a comprehensive review of administrative workflows, with investment in both system improvements and staff training as a matter of urgency.

body.question_10: What are your key recommendations for improving the overall student experience in the coming academic year?
body.answer_10: My key recommendation for the School of Information Technology is to urgently address the insufficient systemic issues that have affected Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning students this semester, rather than treating them as isolated incidents. A structured, transparent student feedback mechanism — where concerns are acknowledged within a set timeframe and outcomes communicated back to students — would significantly rebuild trust that has been eroded by unaddressed issues. I also strongly suggest leadership to conduct a comprehensive curriculum and service quality review in genuine consultation with current students, alumni, and industry partners. Without meaningful action on the concerns raised in this feedback, student satisfaction and the institution's competitive standing will continue to decline in the years ahead.
